entries.label: Version 1
entries.prompt:
Website design system for Wallet Nation, a B2B crypto payment-protection platform. Apply this consistently across all pages and components.

Colors: White (#FFFFFF) background base. Sage green (#D8EECC) as the single accent color for CTAs, icons, highlights, stat callouts, and dividers. Primary text/headlines in near-black (#171717). Secondary/muted text and labels in gray (#6B7280). Borders, card outlines, and dividers in light gray (#E5E7EB). No dark sections; use soft drop shadows instead of glow effects.

Typography: Bold, rounded geometric sans-serif for headlines, oversized with tight line-height. Lighter-weight sans-serif for body copy and labels. Small uppercase eyebrow labels in sage green or muted gray above each section headline.

Buttons: Pill-shaped. Primary = solid sage-green fill with dark text. Secondary = white fill with thin #E5E7EB outline and dark text.

Cards: Rounded corners, #E5E7EB borders, soft drop shadows, white or very light gray background.

Nav: Sticky top nav, logo left, links centered, solid sage-green pill CTA button right.

Tone: Confident, precise, institutional-fintech — B2B infrastructure feel, not consumer crypto hype. Clean, credible, generous whitespace, no flashy neon or gimmicky crypto imagery. Visuals should suggest trust and technical rigor (abstract lock/shield/flow diagrams rather than card mockups).

entries.label: Version 2
entries.prompt:
Build the Wallet Nation homepage using the design system already established. Sections in order:

1. Hero: Headline "Wallet Nation enables payment platforms and merchants to introduce non-custodial payment protection for stablecoin transactions without changing existing payment infrastructure." Primary CTA "Talk to us", secondary CTA "How it works". Below hero, four stat cards: "7 markets — Protected across every use case", "100% — Non-custodial — never hold funds", "Chain-agnostic — USDC, USDT, AUDD, AUDM & more", "Zero-disruption — Drop into existing payment rails".

2. Why It Matters: Eyebrow "WHY IT MATTERS". Headline "Digital asset payments are irreversible by design. Protection should not require a custodian." Body explaining on-chain settlement has no chargeback/reversal, and Wallet Nation closes that gap with a non-custodial conditional-release layer. Pull-quote: "Wallets give control. They don't give protection. — Wallet Nation solves trust."

3. Why Wallet Nation: Eyebrow "WHY WALLET NATION". Headline "Built for digital asset payments that need protection without compromise." Sub "Four pillars that make Wallet Nation the non-custodial protection layer for digital assets." Four cards: Non-custodial ("Protection enforced on-chain through cryptographic control credentials — never through custodial accounts or third-party escrow."); Chain-agnostic ("Works across major digital asset networks. Designed for USDC, USDT, and major stablecoins on any blockchain."); Zero disruption ("Integrates alongside existing payment rails and wallets — no migration, no new custody, no changes to your core stack."); Conditional settlement ("Payment release stays conditional until agreed commercial conditions are confirmed by both parties or a trusted third party.").

4. How It Works: Eyebrow "HOW IT WORKS". Headline "How protected payments work." Sub "Wallet Nation uses Transfer Wallet™ technology to protect digital asset payments by conditionally transferring control — not custody of funds." Five numbered steps: 01 Transfer Wallet created ("A Transfer Wallet is created for the transaction. Cryptographic control credentials are generated. No funds are deposited yet."); 02 Parties receive encrypted control credentials ("Both transacting parties receive their encrypted credentials. Control is shared — no single party can act alone, and Wallet Nation holds no custody."); 03 Funds deposited ("The sender deposits funds into the Transfer Wallet. They remain under the cryptographic control of both parties — not Wallet Nation."); 04 Conditions satisfied → control released ("When agreed conditions are met — delivery confirmed, milestone reached, window expired — control is released and settlement completes on-chain."); 05 Dispute raised → arbitrator resolves ("If a dispute arises, an arbitrator reviews evidence and co-signs a resolution. The arbitrator cannot unilaterally move funds — rules are encoded, not discretionary.").

5. Solutions: Eyebrow "SOLUTIONS". Headline "Protection across every use case." Sub: Wallet Nation serves seven markets. Seven cards, each clickable through to its own detail page: Crypto Commerce ("Protect stablecoin payment rails before settlement becomes final. Merchants and customers complete purchases with greater confidence."); Stablecoin Merchant Payments ("Drop into existing stablecoin payment rails. No wallet migration, no new custody arrangements, no changes to your core stack."); Exchange Funding & Withdrawals ("Reduce funding and withdrawal risk before finality. Introduce a conditional window between instruction and finality."); Embedded Wallet Protection ("Add trusted payment protection to existing wallet experiences without rebuilding your payment stack or disrupting user flows."); High-Value OTC Transfers ("Reduce settlement risk for institutional transfers. Both counterparties protected in the same settlement window."); B2B Digital Asset Settlement ("Bring conditional settlement to enterprise digital asset payments with approval workflows — without changing existing processes."); Web3 Marketplace Transactions ("Protect marketplace transactions before ownership changes. Asset and payment linked — neither party completes without the other performing.").

6. Scale: Eyebrow "SCALE". Headline "Built for the future of digital payments." Sub "Wallet Nation aligns with existing payment infrastructure rather than replacing it. The business process stays the same; the settlement layer improves." Three features: Non-custodial by design ("The protection layer operates on-chain, preserving self-custody principles. No third-party custody, no escrow agent, no additional counterparty risk."); Drop-in integration ("Works alongside existing payment infrastructure — no new rails, no new wallets, no customer re-education. Protection scales with every transaction."); Transfer Wallet™ technology ("Conditionally transfers control — not custody of funds. Protection is enforced through cryptographic credentials.").

7. FAQ: Eyebrow "FAQ". Headline "Frequently asked questions." Accordion with these Q&As:
- What is Wallet Nation? "Wallet Nation is a non-custodial transaction protection platform that introduces conditional settlement for digital asset payments. Rather than holding customer funds, Wallet Nation protects the transaction by controlling the Transfer Wallet™ until agreed conditions have been satisfied."
- How is Wallet Nation different from traditional escrow? "Traditional escrow providers hold customer funds until a transaction is completed. Wallet Nation does not hold customer funds. Instead, it conditionally controls the Transfer Wallet, allowing the transacting parties to remain in control while introducing protection before settlement becomes final."
- Does Wallet Nation take custody of digital assets? "No. Wallet Nation is designed as a non-custodial platform. Customer assets remain under the control of the transacting parties throughout the transaction. Wallet Nation never operates as a custodian of customer funds."
- What is Transfer Wallet™? "Transfer Wallet™ is Wallet Nation's patent-pending transaction mechanism. It enables digital asset transactions to be protected through conditional release rather than irreversible settlement, allowing agreed conditions to be met before control is transferred."
- How does Wallet Nation resolve disputes? "If a dispute arises before settlement conditions have been satisfied, the Transfer Wallet remains protected while the parties seek resolution. Where required, an agreed independent arbitrator can review the evidence and assist in resolving the dispute."
- Does Wallet Nation reverse blockchain transactions? "No. Wallet Nation does not reverse blockchain transactions. Instead, it introduces protection before settlement becomes final, reducing the need for reversals by preventing disputes before they occur."
- Which digital assets are supported? "Wallet Nation is designed to be blockchain and asset agnostic. The architecture can support stablecoins, cryptocurrencies and other tokenised digital assets, depending on implementation by partners."
- Who is Wallet Nation designed for? "Wallet Nation is designed for organisations that facilitate digital asset transactions, including: Payment platforms, Stablecoin providers, Crypto exchanges, Wallet providers, Enterprise payment platforms, Web3 marketplaces, OTC trading platforms."
- Does Wallet Nation replace existing wallets or payment platforms? "No. Wallet Nation is designed to integrate alongside existing wallets, exchanges and payment infrastructure, adding an additional protection layer without requiring organisations to replace their current systems."
- Why is non-custodial architecture important? "Keeping customers in control of their own assets reduces custodial complexity, improves security and preserves the self-sovereign principles of blockchain while still introducing trusted transaction protection. This aligns with the core architectural principles described in the Wallet Nation whitepaper."
- Is Wallet Nation a consumer wallet? "No. Wallet Nation is infrastructure. It is licensed to payment providers, exchanges, wallet platforms and enterprises that want to introduce protected digital asset settlement into their existing products."
- How do I learn more? "If you're exploring protected digital asset payments, licensing opportunities or potential partnerships, contact the Wallet Nation team to discuss your specific use case."

8. Contact: Eyebrow "CONTACT". Headline "How can we help protect your payment flows?" Sub "Tell us about your payment environment. Our team will get back to you with a tailored overview of how Wallet Nation fits your infrastructure." Fields: Name, Company, Email, Message. Button: "Send message".

9. Footer: Tagline "The non-custodial protection layer for digital asset payments." Columns — Solutions (All solutions), Company (FAQ, Contact), Technology (How It Works, Transfer Wallet™). "© 2026 Wallet Nation. All rights reserved."

Responsive: mobile collapses nav to hamburger, stat/pillar/solution grids stack to 1-2 columns, stepper becomes vertical, FAQ stays single column.

entries.label: Version 6
entries.prompt:
Update the existing Wallet Nation design with the following changes only — keep all layout, structure, and copy as-is.

1. Color update: Replace the current flat sage-green (#D8EECC) accent with this green palette instead:
- Primary bright accent: #C9FA49 (neon lime) — use for primary CTA buttons, key highlights, and active states (replacing the sage-green fill).
- Deep secondary green: #4B5A34 (muted olive/moss) — use for secondary button fills, icon chips, card shading, hover states, and gradient depth.
- Light tint green: #EAF7C9 — use sparingly for section background tints or subtle card backgrounds.
Keep the white (#FFFFFF) base background, near-black (#171717) primary text, gray (#6B7280) secondary text, and light-gray (#E5E7EB) borders unchanged.

2. Hero visual: Add a card mockup graphic to the hero — two overlapping payment cards, angled and offset, dark card face fading into the deep-olive (#4B5A34) gradient toward the bottom, white "VISA"-style wordmark, and a bright-lime (#C9FA49) corner block with "WALLET NATION" branding.

3. Partner strip: Add a full-bleed diagonal ribbon band (bright lime fading toward deep olive) beneath the hero, displaying a row of recognizable payment/crypto brand logos in dark (#171717) text/icon form — e.g. Binance, Coinbase, Stripe, PayPal, and one additional payments brand — evenly spaced and consistently sized.

Note: the card mockup and partner logos are placeholder/demo styling for this practice build only — swap for verified real partner logos or generic marks before any live/public launch.

entries.label: Version 7

entries.label: Version 8
entries.prompt:
Replace every instance of the current green (#D8EECC sage green) across the entire site — every page, every component, every state — with the following palette. Do not leave any element using the old sage green. This applies globally, not just to new sections.

Find and replace throughout:
- Any button, pill, or CTA currently using sage green (#D8EECC) → change fill to bright lime (#C9FA49).
- Any icon, chip, badge, eyebrow label, or small accent currently using sage green → change to deep olive (#4B5A34).
- Any card background, section background, or tint currently using sage green or a pale green tint → change to pale lime tint (#EAF7C9).
- Any border, outline, or divider currently using a green tone → change to deep olive (#4B5A34) or light gray (#E5E7EB) as appropriate, matching how it's used elsewhere on the page.
- Any hover/active state currently using sage green → change to the corresponding new green (bright lime, deep olive, or pale tint) based on its role above.

Check every page (homepage, all seven solution detail pages, FAQ, contact) and every section (hero, stat cards, feature blocks, step numbers, solution cards, pricing/benefit cards, footer links, nav CTA) individually — confirm none of them still reference the old sage green (#D8EECC) anywhere before finishing.

Do not change layout, copy, images, icons content, or logos — this is a color-only pass, but it must be applied completely and consistently across 100% of the site, with zero remaining instances of the old sage green.

entries.label: Version 9
entries.prompt:
Add a new "Our Members" section to the Wallet Nation homepage, placed after the "Scale" section and before "FAQ." Match this structure and style:

Eyebrow label: "OUR TEAM" (bright lime #C9FA49, uppercase, centered).
Headline: "Get to know the people behind Wallet Nation" (centered, bold geometric sans-serif, near-black #171717).
Sub-line: "Our mission is to build the trusted non-custodial protection layer for digital asset payments — empowering platforms to settle with confidence." (centered, muted gray #6B7280).

Grid of 6 team member cards, 3 columns on desktop, 1 column on mobile. Each card: a portrait photo cut out against a solid bright-lime (#C9FA49) background block, rounded corners, consistent sizing across all cards. Beneath each photo block: name in bold near-black text, title/role beneath in muted gray (#6B7280).

Placeholder team members (replace with real names/photos later):
- Alex Morgan — CEO of Wallet Nation
- Jamie Chen — Head of Product
- Sam Okafor — Lead Blockchain Engineer
- Priya Anand — Head of Partnerships
- Jordan Lee — UX Designer
- Taylor Brooks — Head of Compliance

Keep the rest of the page's white background and existing color system intact — only this section's photo-card background uses the bright-lime block treatment, matching the reference site's "Our Members" card style.

entries.label: Version 11
entries.prompt:
Typography: Use "Poppins" (Google Font) for all text on the site, no secondary font.
- Headlines (H1/H2): Poppins, weight 600 (semi-bold), large scale (e.g. 56–90px depending on section prominence), tight line-height (roughly 1.1–1.3x font size), color #171717.
- Sub-headlines/section intros: Poppins, weight 600, smaller scale (e.g. 28–36px).
- Body copy: Poppins, weight 400, 15–16px, 1.5–1.6x line-height, color #6B7280 for secondary/muted text, #171717 for primary body text.
- Eyebrow labels: Poppins, weight 600, uppercase, small size (12–13px), letter-spacing slightly increased (e.g. 0.5px), color bright lime (#C9FA49) or deep olive (#4B5A34).
- Buttons: Poppins, weight 500–600, 15–16px.
Load Poppins from Google Fonts if not already available in the platform's font library; do not substitute a different sans-serif.

entries.label: Version 12
entries.prompt:
Add a section after "Scale" (or before "Our Members" if that's included) with a two-column layout. Left column: headline "Built on infrastructure you can trust" (Poppins, weight 600, near-black #171717), sub-copy "Wallet Nation is designed to scale across markets, transaction volumes, and jurisdictions without compromising protection." (Poppins, weight 400, #6B7280), and a "Talk to us" button (bright lime #C9FA49 pill).
Right column: three overlapping stat cards, angled/offset like a stacked deck, white cards with #E5E7EB border and soft shadow (not black/dark like the reference — keep on-brand with the light theme), large bold numbers in deep olive (#4B5A34) or near-black, label beneath each in muted gray. Placeholder stats (replace with real figures later): "500M+ — Transaction volume protected", "100+ — Countries supported", "7 — Markets served".

Add a testimonials section with a two-column layout. Left column: customer headshot photo in a rounded rectangle frame. Right column: eyebrow label "TESTIMONIALS" (bright lime #C9FA49, uppercase), headline "What our partners are saying" (Poppins weight 600, near-black), a quote in Poppins weight 400 (#171717), and name + title beneath in bold near-black / muted gray. Include left/right arrow navigation buttons (circular, outline style, #E5E7EB border) to cycle between multiple testimonials.

Placeholder testimonial (replace with real customer quote later):
"Wallet Nation gave us a way to introduce payment protection without touching a single line of our existing settlement infrastructure. That mattered enormously for our compliance timeline."
— Placeholder Name, Placeholder Title, Placeholder Company

entries.label: Version 13
entries.prompt:
Implement dynamic rotating copy in the homepage Hero section.

Do not redesign the Hero.

Do not change the layout.

Do not change the navigation.

Do not change the CTA buttons.

Do not change the illustration.

Only implement the rotating Hero headline.

====================================================

HEADLINE

The Hero headline consists of two lines.

The FIRST line rotates.

The SECOND line never changes.

====================================================

ROTATING FIRST LINE

Rotate through these seven messages in this exact order:

Stablecoins solve volatility.

Crypto made payments instant.

Exchanges move funds faster.

Wallets put users in control.

OTC desks move millions.

B2B settlement is evolving.

Marketplaces connect buyers and sellers.

====================================================

FIXED SECOND LINE

Always display:

Wallet Nation solves trust.

This line must never animate.

====================================================

DISPLAY EXAMPLES

Stablecoins solve volatility.
Wallet Nation solves trust.

Crypto made payments instant.
Wallet Nation solves trust.

Exchanges move funds faster.
Wallet Nation solves trust.

Wallets put users in control.
Wallet Nation solves trust.

OTC desks move millions.
Wallet Nation solves trust.

B2B settlement is evolving.
Wallet Nation solves trust.

Marketplaces connect buyers and sellers.
Wallet Nation solves trust.

====================================================

ANIMATION

Use a smooth fade transition.

Fade out.

Replace first line.

Fade in.

Transition duration:

400ms

Display each message for:

3.5 seconds

Loop continuously.

Pause animation when the browser tab is inactive.

Respect prefers-reduced-motion.

If reduced motion is enabled, display only:

Stablecoins solve volatility.
Wallet Nation solves trust.

====================================================

LAYOUT

Reserve enough vertical height so the Hero never changes size during rotation.

Do not move the illustration.

Do not move the CTA buttons.

Do not create any layout shift.

====================================================

FINAL QA

Confirm:

✓ Only the first line rotates.

✓ The second line always remains "Wallet Nation solves trust."

✓ Messages rotate in the correct order.

✓ Smooth fade transition only.

✓ No typing animation.

✓ No sliding animation.

✓ No layout jump.

✓ Hero design remains otherwise unchanged.

- Name in a distinct italic/serif-style font beneath the illustration.
- Title beneath the name ("Co-Founder").
- A thin divider line.
- An expandable "More" toggle (with a small minus/plus icon) revealing a short bio paragraph.
- A second thin divider line beneath the bio.
- A circular outline LinkedIn icon button beneath that, linking to their profile.

Team members:
1. Angela Clark — Co-Founder — "Likes digital products that work beautifully. Hopeful for the Web3 era. Has worked in financial services, fintech and media. Enjoys nature and talks too quickly."
2. Ric Richardson — Co-Founder — "Likes inventing things, and often. Dedicated to helping unlock the benefits of Web3 for everyone. Is well known for his invention of software activation. Enjoys music and has too many guitars."

Keep the overall site's white background, Poppins typography, and #171717/#6B7280 text colors; use bright lime (#C9FA49) or deep olive (#4B5A34) only for small accent details in the illustrations and the LinkedIn icon outline.

entries.label: Version 20
entries.prompt:
The "Scale" section (and check any other similarly-styled sections) is still using the old sage green (#D8EECC) — confirm this exact hex is not used anywhere and replace every instance with the new palette as follows:

1. Illustration card background: change from green to plain white (#FFFFFF), with a thin #E5E7EB border or soft drop shadow so it visually separates from the section background behind it. Do not tint this card green at all.

2. Section band background: reduce the pale tint (#EAF7C9) to a much subtler application — light enough that it reads as a soft off-white-green wash, not a solid saturated green fill. If the current tint looks too strong/saturated, lighten it further (e.g. blend it much closer to white) rather than using a flat solid fill at full strength.

3. Do not apply green tint to both the section background and the card inside it — pick one: either a very subtle tinted section background with a white card on top for contrast, or a white section background with no tint at all. The current result (green section + green card, same shade) has zero contrast and looks like a solid color block — this needs visual separation between background and card.

Audit every section using this same banded-background + illustration-card pattern across the whole site and apply this same correction consistently.

Each card: a portrait photo cutout on a solid bright-lime (#C9FA49) rounded-corner background block, consistent sizing across all 3 cards.
